pluginDescription: Keep watch for priority stations while fully idle, then lock and call them automatically on the next transmit slot.
watchOverviewDesc: Use this for DX, friends, rare entities, or scheduled skeds you do not want to miss. It is disabled by default; once the current operator has watch rules, the plugin listens only in pure standby and reuses the existing requestCall flow to lock the target and align transmission with the next slot.
watchListDesc: One rule per line, checked in order. Rules use the match mode below; legacy lines containing regex syntax such as ^, $, or .* are still treated as regex rules for compatibility. Lines starting with # are kept as comments and ignored. Leave empty to disable this operator. Already-worked stations are skipped within the configured number of days.
watchListInvalidRegexFormat: Regex format is invalid on line {{line}}. Please check the expression.
watchListInvalidRegexSyntax: Regex syntax cannot be parsed on line {{line}}. Check brackets, escaping, or quantifiers.
triggerModeDesc: Choose which kinds of appearances should trigger autocall. All modes also include messages addressed directly to your callsign.
autocallPriorityDesc: Higher values win when multiple automatic-call plugins match in the same slot.
workedCallsignSkipDaysDesc: Stations worked within this many days are automatically skipped and will not be called again. Set to 0 to disable this check.
removeExactMatchAfterQSODesc: When enabled, explicitly listed callsigns (not wildcards or prefixes) are automatically removed from the watch list after a QSO completes, preventing repeated calls.
watchMatchModeDesc: Exact or prefix matching is best for most operators. Fuzzy matching finds partial callsigns. Advanced regex is for users already comfortable with regular expressions.
